stocks.note: Numbers as of market close March 26th
stocks.news:
A California jury found that Meta's Instagram and Google's YouTube were designed to be too addictive for younger users, sending shares of Meta (META -7.92%) and Google (GOOG -3.06%) sharply lower. The verdict follows a similar ruling in a New Mexico court and could set the stage for a wave of additional lawsuits against social media companies.

Chipmakers also had a rough session, with Intel (INTC -6.53%) and AMD (AMD -7.49%) both falling despite announcing plans to raise chip prices.

economy.commentary:
Goldman Sachs estimates the oil-price surge triggered by the U.S.-Iran conflict could suppress payroll growth by roughly 10,000 jobs per month through year-end, with restaurants, hotels, and retail bearing the brunt of reduced consumer spending.

On the labor front, initial unemployment claims came in lower than expected at 210,000 last week, and continuing claims fell to their lowest level in nearly two years, suggesting companies are holding on to workers even as energy costs squeeze margins.

crypto.commentary:
Fannie Mae is partnering with Better Home & Finance and Coinbase to accept crypto-backed mortgages for the first time. The program lets homebuyers pledge Bitcoin and other cryptocurrencies as collateral for their down payment, eliminating the need to sell holdings to purchase a home.

Meanwhile, Marathon Digital (MARA +3.62%) saw shares climb after unloading over $1 billion in Bitcoin to pay down debt at a discount, while Binance's market dominance continues to erode, now controlling just 27% of Bitcoin trading volume.

real_estate.homebuilder: Construction of Frederick Airport Park, a 509,000-square-foot industrial development featuring two buildings, was completed this quarter in Frederick, Maryland.
real_estate.commentary:
The Bay Area market is "quietly accelerating" as it enters the spring season, with inventory beginning to expand and buyer activity picking up despite higher price sensitivity in some areas.

📍 Latest Real Estate News in the Bay Area: March data reveals a true "selective rebound" in the regional market. Santa Clara County single-family home prices have reached a median of $2,000,000, up 0.63% year-over-year, while the condo market in the same area saw a sharper decline of nearly 11%. Alameda County single-family sales (excluding condos) grew by 10.5%, signaling strong demand for detached homes as inventory expands slightly heading into April. Competition remains fierce in seller-favored zones, with Santa Clara homes selling in a median of just 8 days.

📍 Latest General News in the Bay Area: In Fremont, the Police Department lobby is operating on modified hours through today, March 27, due to staff training sessions. In Santa Clara County, the State Assembly recently voted unanimously to establish Farmworkers Day in California, a bipartisan bill co-authored by local representative Speaker Robert Rivas. Additionally, BART is launching an online tool to generate revenue by renting underutilized parking spots to local businesses across the system, including stations in the South Bay.
